meta.title: Platform Lifecycle Intelligence Production Certification & Enterprise Integration
honesty_contract: Never fabricate, never estimate; repository overrides assumptions

pre_integration_audit.note: Each tier verified by repository presence (service/route/migration), route registration, flag presence, and a defensive read-only getter probe. getter_ready reflects DB substrate (built ≠ populated).

part1_integration.note: Integrated = route file present AND registered in routes.ts. Composability separately probed via each tier getter (Integrated ≠ Activated; getter_ready=false is honest dormant substrate, not an integration failure).

part2_hardening.note: Stability scores are MEASURED by the 1.41 Automation engine and surfaced SEPARATELY (no composite). null = unmeasurable (substrate absent), never 0.

part3_enterprise_certification.note: Each component certified STRUCTURAL when its canonical implementation is present; substrate_ready reports whether the DB substrate is populated (Available ≠ Operational). Certified ≠ Production-Ready.

backward_compatibility.basis: Every tier flag defaults OFF → flag-OFF path is byte-identical legacy (structural; not runtime-diffed).

forward_compatibility.basis: New tiers are additive behind OFF flags; enabling is opt-in (structural).

migration_compatibility.basis: Each persisting tier ships a canonical forward-only migration file (structural presence).

database_compatibility.basis: Tiers use additive tables + lazy ensure-schema on write paths only; reads to_regclass-probe (structural).

api_compatibility.basis: Each tier owns a distinct BASE path; no existing route signatures modified (structural).

module_compatibility.basis: Each tier is a distinct service module composed, not forked (structural).

feature_flag_compatibility.basis: All lifecycle flags present and default OFF (structural).

part4_compatibility.note: Compatibility here is STRUCTURALLY verified (repository + flag defaults), NOT runtime regression-tested. Validated ≠ Production-Ready.

part5_repository_certification.note: Integrity / consistency / completeness are MEASURED by the 1.37 + 1.39 read getters; null reflects absent substrate (built ≠ populated), never fabricated 0.

part6_performance.note: No load-testing tooling is available in this environment, so throughput / percentile latency are NOT MEASURED (null, never estimated). Only the wall-clock latency of composing all tier getters (run once, in parallel) was measured.

part7_security.note: Governance/compliance ratios are MEASURED by the 1.41 engine (Policy-Exists ≠ Compliant; per-domain ratios SEPARATE). Administration security verified by the cert route requiring auth + super-admin behind the flag gate.

no_duplicate_registries.evidence: Single canonical Foundation registry service; no parallel registry file detected.

no_business_logic_change.evidence: Phase 1.43 ships only a read-only certification composer + probe route; it composes existing getters and performs NO writes/DDL.

no_dormant_capability_activation.evidence: All lifecycle tier flags (1.37–1.43) default OFF; certification reports the dormant substrate honestly rather than activating it.

part8_quality.note: Quality is MEASURED by repository scan + flag defaults. Built-but-OFF tiers are reported as dormant, NOT as activated and NOT as technical debt.

part9_production_readiness.reason: Structural integration, quality and compatibility are CERTIFIED, but Production-Ready is WITHHELD by design: this internal lifecycle subsystem has no runtime adoption + realized-outcome evidence, and structural certification must never be composited into a production-confidence claim.
part9_production_readiness.note: The four axes are reported SEPARATELY and never blended. production_confidence = null (not 0) because it is unmeasurable here, not because it is zero.

lifecycle_certification.basis: Registry / catalog / metadata / intelligence / evolution / automation / governance present.

architecture_certification.basis: Additive composition; no business-logic change; module compatibility structural.

enterprise_readiness.basis: Tiers integrated; administration secured (auth + super-admin behind flag gate).

production_readiness.basis: Structural integration, quality and compatibility are CERTIFIED, but Production-Ready is WITHHELD by design: this internal lifecycle subsystem has no runtime adoption + realized-outcome evidence, and structural certification must never be composited into a production-confidence claim.

part10_final_certification.note: Five reports certify STRUCTURAL completeness; Production Readiness is WITHHELD — Integrated ≠ Certified ≠ Production-Ready.

verdict.reason: Structural integration, quality and compatibility are CERTIFIED, but Production-Ready is WITHHELD by design: this internal lifecycle subsystem has no runtime adoption + realized-outcome evidence, and structural certification must never be composited into a production-confidence claim.
